description: o key: take the selected Issue, construct the Linear URL (https://linear.app/issue/<identifier>), call open::that(url). r key: re-run the GraphQL query with the same args, replace App.issues, reset selected index and scroll offset, clear any error state. Show a brief 'Loading...' state while the request is in flight.
close_reason: o key opens https://linear.app/issue/<identifier> via open crate; r key calls refresh() with loading/error state

title: tui: vim-style half/full page scroll
description:
Add Ctrl+D (half page down), Ctrl+U (half page up), Ctrl+F / PageDown (full page down), Ctrl+B / PageUp (full page up).

Store viewport_height: u16 in App. Update it in ui::render_table() each frame: viewport_height = table_area.height.saturating_sub(1) (subtract table header row).

In event handler:
  Ctrl+D -> move_by(+(viewport_height/2))
  Ctrl+U -> move_by(-(viewport_height/2))  (saturating)
  Ctrl+F / PageDown -> move_by(+viewport_height)
  Ctrl+B / PageUp   -> move_by(-viewport_height)

move_by(delta: i32): clamp new index to 0..issues.len()-1, call table_state.select(Some(new_i)).
close_reason: Ctrl+d/u half-page, Ctrl+f/b + PageDown/Up full-page. viewport_height set in render_table each frame. move_by(delta) with i32 clamp.

title: tui: / filter overlay (stretch)
description:
/ key opens a single-line input bar at the bottom (replaces footer while active). Characters append to input buffer, Backspace deletes, Enter applies, Esc dismisses without change.

Add title: Option<String> to IssueArgs (long flag --title) and handle it in filter.rs as { title: { containsIgnoreCase: value } }.

App gets: input_mode: bool, input_buf: String, and fetches with app.args.title = Some(input_buf) on Enter. On Esc, restore previous title and re-fetch only if title changed. Show prompt '/ ' followed by cursor in footer area while input_mode is active.

Filter overlay should compose with all existing CLI filters (team/state/assignee/etc) -- it is additive.
close_reason: / opens input overlay, Enter applies title:containsIgnoreCase filter, Esc dismisses. input_mode + input_buf on App, footer replaced by '/ buf_' prompt while active.

title: FINDING: client-api.linear.app rejects OAuth tokens
description:
Empirically verified 2026-02-25 via lt sync probe.

GET https://client-api.linear.app/sync/bootstrap?type=full&onlyModels=Issue
Authorization: Bearer <oauth_token>

Response: HTTP 403
{
  "error": "OAuth token authentication not allowed on this endpoint",
  "extensions": {
    "type": "forbidden",
    "code": "FORBIDDEN",
    "statusCode": 403,
    "userPresentableMessage": "OAuth token authentication not allowed on this endpoint"
  }
}

Conclusion: Linear explicitly gates client-api.linear.app to browser session auth.
OAuth tokens (including those from the documented public OAuth2 flow) are refused.
The "local-first via Linear sync protocol" approach is not viable.

Alternatives:
  1. Public GraphQL + periodic polling + local SQLite cache (recommended)
  2. Webhooks (requires reachable server, not suitable for a TUI)
  3. Personal API tokens -- likely same result since the check is on auth METHOD
     not auth credential type. Could be tested but low probability of success.
close_reason: Finding documented. Pivot to GraphQL polling + local cache.
